title: 10 best herbal capsules for runny nose

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with runny nose because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The capsules also contain antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can contribute to chronic sinusitis and upper respiratory infections. Additionally, the herb has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from a runny nose and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: The bioactive compounds in Kansas Coneflower, such as cichoric acid and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit histamine release, which can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas Coneflower contains a variety of flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce swelling and excess mucus production in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Immune system modulation: The saponins and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower have been found to modulate the immune response, increasing the production of cytokines that help to fight off infections and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms, including a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas conefeower plant are used to make medicinal capsules for runny nose because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce congestion and soothe the nasal passages.
Buds: The buds of the Kansas coneflower plant are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have natural antihistamine properties that can help alleviate runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots and flowers in a clean environment.
Clean and dry the harvested material by spreading it out under shade for several days.
Crush 20 grams of the cleaned echinacea into fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 50 grams of gelatin capsules in a glass container for 10 minutes.
Fill each capsule with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with runny nose because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that calm and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages. These capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a reduced flow of mucus. Additionally, maidenhair tree has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that help to combat infections that can cause runny nose, providing long-lasting relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Ginkgo biloba capsules have antihistamine effects, which can help to reduce histamine release in the body and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The extracts of Maidenhair Tree capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Expectorant properties: Ginkgo biloba's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial properties, roots help to reduce inflammation and combat infection in the nasal passages.
Barks: Containing natural astringents, barks constrict blood vessels and reduce congestion, providing relief from runny noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning when they are still dry measuring approximately 20-25 grams.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within minutes.
Mix the powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 grams of gelatin to create a blend of 10% herb and 90% filler measuring approximately 120-130 grams.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the ginkgo biloba-gelatin blend using a capsule filling machine or manually pouring the mixture within 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with runny nose because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry, which soothes and calms irritated nasal passages. The anthocyanins in elderberry have been shown to reduce swelling and ease congestion, providing fast and effective relief from sinus pressure and runny noses. Additionally, elderberry's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that can cause excess mucus production, allowing the body to naturally heal and recover.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Elderberry's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help reduce nasal inflammation and congestion, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Decongestant and Anti-Histamine Effects: The bioactive compounds in elderberry capsules, like quercetin, possess decongestant and anti-histamine properties that can help shrink blood vessels, reducing nasal swelling and excess mucus production.

Immune System Support: Elderberry's immunomodulatory effects can help stimulate the immune system to produce cytokines, which in turn, support the body's natural defense against viral and bacterial infections that may contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, elder roots help reduce congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes contain expectorant properties that loosen mucus and promote its expulsion from the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and stems in early summer, ensuring sufficient quantity for preparation.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or trays, leaving them undisturbed for 7-10 days.
Grind 30g of dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, taking care to avoid over-processing.
Mix the powdered herb with 100ml of vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.

Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and filling gelatin capsules with the resulting tincture at a dose of 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The herb stevia, also known as sweet leaf, has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as congestion and sinusitis. When taken in capsule form, it can help to dry up excess mucus and alleviate discomfort associated with a runny nose. Additionally, stevia capsules may also help to boost the immune system, further supporting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Stevia's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds present in stevia capsules have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help to reduce swelling and mucus production in the nasal tissues, leading to a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: Stevia's saponins can help thin and loosen mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from a runny nose and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, stevia roots help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Buds: Containing essential oils and saponins, stevia buds have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried stevia rebaudiana leaves 500mg, gelatin capsules and water.
Grind dried stevia rebaudiana leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix ground stevia powder with water to form a paste consistency adding one teaspoon of water at a time for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules by spooning the stevia paste mixture into empty capsules and sealing tightly.
Label and store the herbal stevia capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with runny nose because they contain a concentrated extract of thymol, a natural antiseptic compound that has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which helps to alleviate congestion and runny nose caused by colds, allergies, and other conditions. Additionally, thyme's antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, help reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to a runny nose and sinus pressure.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause a runny nose, including colds, flu, and allergies, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, thyme roots help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Buds: Containing potent flavonoids, thyme buds aid in soothing inflammation and reducing excessive mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Dry thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer on paper bags for 7-10 days or until brittle.
Grind dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of powdered thymus vulgaris with 500 milligrams of gelatin and 20 milliliters of water to form a mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the thyme mixture and seal them immediately.
Store filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo capsules helps with runny nose because of its unique ability to combat inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The natural ingredients, including polyphenols and saponins, work together to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure, allowing for a smoother flow of mucus and a significant decrease in runniness. Additionally, umckaloabo's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, providing long-lasting relief from chronic congestion and sinus issues.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Umckaloabo capsules have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, reducing the severity and duration of respiratory infections, including those that cause runny noses.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can help alleviate congestion and discharge associated with a runny nose.
Mucolytic properties: Umckaloabo's mucolytic agents break down excess mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the thickness and stickiness of nasal secretions, allowing for clearer breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to provide a concentrated source of bioactive compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain active compounds that help to combat inflammation and infection, making them effective in alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium sidoides roots dig up mature plants during optimal weather conditions ensuring quality material.
Cleanse the harvested roots thoroughly rinse with water remove dirt debris gently scrub with soft brush.
Dry the cleaned roots air dry for 7 to 10 days or use a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours ensure complete desiccation.
Grind dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder sift through mesh screen to remove any lumps or large particles.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered pelargonium sidoides root mix one part herb to two parts filler material such as lactose or potato starch store in airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with runny nose because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages. These acids also exhibit mucolytic activity, breaking down excess mucus and making it easier to expel. As a result, frankincense capsules may provide relief from constant runny nose, sinus pressure, and postnasal drip, helping to promote clear breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense capsules reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and runny nose by reducing swelling and irritation.

Antihistamine properties: Frankincense contains natural antihistamines that can help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and subsequent runny nose, sneezing, and itching.

Antimicrobial properties: Frankincense capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and viruses, which can help to combat infections that may contribute to a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of frankincense contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including incensole and incensyl acetate, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.

Barks: Frankincense barks have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including runny nose, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and grind 300mg of dried boswellia serrata powder into fine particles.
Combine the ground powder with 500mg of gelatin and 100mg of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Add 10ml of purified water to the mixture while stirring continuously for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into empty vegetable capsules and seal immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden tulip capsules helps with runny nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the nasal passages. Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in tulip capsules can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, further reducing the severity of nasal congestion and runniness.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tulipa gesneriana flowers on sunny mornings when they are in full bloom and store them in airtight containers.

Crush 20 grams of dried tulipa gesneriana flowers into fine powders using mortar and pestle for easy infusion.
Mix 5 grams of crushed powder with 100ml of boiling water, steeping for 10 minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled infused liquid, sealing tightly and storing in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with runny nose because they contain a concentrated form of the calming and soothing essential oil, linalool. Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants, allergens, and irritants, which can exacerbate a runny nose.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it effective in relieving nasal discharge.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia in quantities sufficient for preparation.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 250-300 milligrams of lavender powder with 50-100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or saucepan set over low heat for approximately one hour stirring occasionally.
Fill empty capsules with the infused oil and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with runny nose because they contain Eucalyptus globulus, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The capsules' active ingredient, eucalyptol, is released into the body where it breaks down excess mucus, reducing its viscosity and promoting drainage. Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' active compounds, such as cineole and a-terpineol, have anti-inflammatory effects that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce nasal congestion and ease runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help alleviate sinus pressure and relieve nasal discharge.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted source weighing approximately 250 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days or until brittle.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle containing 50 grams of powdered eucalyptus globulus.
Mix the eucalyptus globulus powder with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal extract and seal tightly storing them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with runny nose because it contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called calendula, which effectively reduces swelling in the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning when they are fully open and free of dew.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 12-14 hours or by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Crush the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care not to generate excessive heat.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered calendula with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of filtered water to create a uniform paste, allowing it to sit for 10-15 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the paste and seal immediately, resulting in 20-25 capsules that can be stored in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with runny nose because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main active compound, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving symptoms of a runny nose.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 30 grams of potato starch in a clean glass bowl for 5 minutes until well combined.
Fill empty #0 gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal tightly using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 6 months.
Dispense 2-3 capsules orally twice daily as needed to help alleviate runny nose symptoms with a glass of water or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with runny nose because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The diuretic properties of dandelion also aid in draining excess mucus from the sinuses, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can contribute to a runny nose.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.

Grind dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix ground taraxacum officinale with filler material such as silica gel or potato starch in a 1:2 ratio by weight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules help with runny nose because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the nasal passages. When the body produces excess mucus to combat infection or allergies, turmeric's potent bioactive compounds work to thin and clear it out, relieving congestion and discomfort. Regular supplementation can also strengthen the immune system, helping to prevent future episodes of runny nose and sinusitis. remedy_properties: Curcumin: A potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
Catechins: Antioxidant compounds that combat free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the nasal tissues, which can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, thereby reducing runniness.
Fiber: Soluble fiber in turmeric capsules helps to thicken mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the amount of mucus produced, thus alleviating a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help alleviate congestion and nasal discharge.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric rhizomes and clean them thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Grind the cleaned turmeric rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the powdered turmeric with 250mg of pure vegetable capsule material and fill the capsules using a capsule filling machine within 1 hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 2 years.
Take 2-3 capsules orally twice a day with warm water after meals for 6 weeks to alleviate runny nose symptoms naturally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with runny nose because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Measure precisely 500 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a clean glass container for processing.

Mix 250 milliliters of boiling distilled water with the powdered cinnamomum verum to create an infusion for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and allow it to cool at room temperature.

Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 4 grams of the ground herb with 96 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil in capsules helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate congestion and relieve a runny nose.

Decongestant properties: The menthol present in peppermint oil has natural decongestant properties that help to shrink blood vessels in the nasal passages, reducing swelling and excess mucus production.

Antihistamine-like properties: Peppermint oil has been shown to have antihistamine-like effects, which can help to reduce histamine levels in the body, thereby alleviating allergic reactions such as runny nose and itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to thin out mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, reducing redness and swelling associated with runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Dry 20g of fresh peppermint leaves in a low-temperature oven at 35°c for 2 hours.
Grind 10g of dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of powdered peppermint with 250mg of cornstarch and 50mg of magnesium stearate in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill 60-capsule capsules with the herbal mixture and seal tightly for 1 minute.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Treetop hydrangea capsules helps with runny nose because they contain powerful natural ingredients that work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. The capsules' unique blend of herbs, including goldenseal and slippery elm, helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and relieving constant sniffles and dripping. Additionally, treetop hydrangea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the sinuses, providing fast and effective relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory effects: Some studies suggest that Hydrangea root extract has antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate symptoms of allergic rhinitis, such as a runny nose, by reducing histamine release and inflammation in the nasal passages.

Mucolytic activity: Hydrangea root extract has been shown to have mucolytic activity, meaning it can break down mucus and make it easier to expel, which could help relieve congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
Antioxidant properties: Hydrangea root extract contains antioxidants that may help protect the nasal passages from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing symptoms of a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa, reducing runny nose episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hydrangea arborescens leaves in sufficient quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or oven drying at low temperatures.
Grind the dried hydrangea arborescens into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of ground hydrangea arborescens with 1/2 teaspoon of vegetable glycerin and 1/2 cup of distilled water.
Encapsulate the mixture in size zero hard gelatin capsules for a total daily dose of three capsules, taken thrice daily.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for runny nose are natural supplements made from a combination of herbs, botanicals, and other plant-based ingredients designed to alleviate congestion, sinus pressure, and excess mucus production. These capsules work by soothing and decongesting the nasal passages, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy respiratory function. Examples include Echinacea, Quercetin, and N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC), which have been shown to reduce nasal discharge, congestion, and sinus pressure, ultimately improving quality of life for those suffering from chronic runny noses. By using these herbal capsules, individuals can experience relief from constant sneezing, stuffy nose, and fatigue, allowing them to breathe easier and enjoy daily activities without interruption.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal capsules is to consume a blend of Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Slippery Elm. These herbs have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms associated with a runny nose. Take 2-3 capsules twice daily for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and pharyngitis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like goldenseal, echinacea, and marshmallow root help alleviate congestion, inflammation, and respiratory issues by reducing mucus production, soothing the throat, and opening up airways. They also provide antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses.
